payload.summary: This week's review pulse highlights user concerns and praises across various themes. Reliability and Performance (30%) saw issues with syncing, slow downloads, and app hangs. Feature Enhancements and Requests (25%) included demands for a Kill Switch, restore of order history, and addition of Slice Credit Card bill payments. User Experience and Support (20%) faced problems with UPI transactions, harmful app warnings, and poor customer support. Financial Transactions and Security (25%) had complaints about brokerages, stop-loss feature unreliability, and data privacy concerns.
quotes: I love the app! But now download faster and more stable. The app is soo much faster download. Thank you so much to everyone algorithms.
Worst INDmoney's stop-loss feature is unreliable — my CNC stop-loss order was cancelled at the vendor level without any valid reason, causing me a loss of approximately Rs. 7,000.
Great App. Very efficient and reliable. Good work team.
action_items: Optimize app performance to reduce syncing issues and improve download speeds
Enhance customer support by providing timely resolutions and clear communication
Review and refine financial transaction features, including stop-loss and brokerage, to improve user trust and satisfaction
action_ideas: Optimize app performance to reduce syncing issues and improve download speeds
Enhance customer support by providing timely resolutions and clear communication
Review and refine financial transaction features, including stop-loss and brokerage, to improve user trust and satisfaction

explanation_bullets: A fee charged by mutual funds when investors redeem their units within a specified period
The exit load is deducted from the redemption proceeds
The purpose of an exit load is to discourage investors from withdrawing their money too quickly
Exit loads vary across mutual fund schemes and can range from 0.5% to 3% or more
The exit load period can range from a few days to several years
It is typically mentioned in the mutual fund's offer document and key information memorandum
A brokerage fee is a charge levied by a broker or brokerage firm for facilitating a transaction, such as buying or selling securities.
The fee is typically a percentage of the transaction amount or a flat fee per transaction.
Brokerage fees can vary depending on the type of transaction, the broker or brokerage firm, and the market or exchange.
These fees are usually deducted from the transaction amount or paid separately by the client.
Brokerage fees can be applicable to various investment products, including stocks, bonds, mutual funds, and exchange-traded funds (ETFs).
The fees are a source of revenue for brokers and brokerage firms, compensating them for their services and expertise.

I love🍁 the app! But now download faster and more stable. The app is soo much 🫖faster download. Thank you so much to everyone algorithms. This is syncing😘 happens pretty much every time you open the app, start, stop, or edit an entry, so it was a real pain. Not to mention being unable to use the app when it's syncing because it literally stops responding until it's done. You'd stop an entry and it'd be several seconds before it'd actually stop and those extra seconds are added too the entry.
There is everything perfect in this trading app but only one problem I noticed that there is no option like - Kill Switch. I would give 5 star when this feature is enabled.
